pin.noActiveFile: No active file to add as a shortcut.
launcher.title: Saropa Launcher
launcher.empty: No shortcuts yet. Add a file as a shortcut, then it shows here and in the Shortcuts view.
linePin.label: {name}:{line}
linePin.added: Added {name} at line {line}. Opening it jumps back here.
tail.enabled: Following {name} like tail -f. Opening it scrolls to new lines as the file grows.
tail.fileOnly: {name} is not a file shortcut; log follow applies to file shortcuts only.
mask.label: Protected file
mask.tooltip: Masked shortcut. Opening it asks to reveal the file first, so it never appears from a stray click.
mask.revealDetail: This shortcut is masked to avoid showing a sensitive file by accident. Choose Reveal to open it.
mask.on: Masked {name}. Its name is hidden and opening it now asks to reveal first.
mask.off: Revealed {name}. It shows its name again and opens without a prompt.
mask.fileOnly: {name} is not a file shortcut; masking applies to file shortcuts only.
mask.unsupported: {name} is an auto/recipe shortcut and cannot be masked. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
scratch.placeholder: Pick a format for the scratchpad (it never touches disk)
scratch.created: Opened a {format} scratchpad. It lives in memory only — nothing is written to disk or git until you save it.
ghost.none: No commands found that you have typed at least {count} times. The scan reads your local shell history only and is never transmitted.
ghost.placeholder: Pick the commands to save as global shell shortcuts
ghost.added: Saved {count} command(s) as global shell shortcuts. They never run on their own — double-click to run.
env.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder to switch .env profiles.
env.noProfiles: No .env profiles found. Create .env.staging, .env.prod, etc. to switch between them.
env.folderPlaceholder: Pick the folder whose .env to switch
env.placeholder: Pick the environment to make active (.env)
env.unsavedConfirm: Your current .env has changes that match no profile. Back it up to .env.bak and switch to {name}?
env.switched: Switched .env to {name}. Restart your dev server to apply the new environment.
layout.empty: No text editors are open to save as a layout.
layout.saved: Saved layout {name} ({count} editor(s)). Restore it from the Shortcuts toolbar.
layout.none: No saved layouts yet. Arrange your editors, then Save Editor Layout.
layout.restoredPartial: Restored layout {name}: opened {opened}, skipped {failed} that no longer exist.
pin.missingTooltip: File not found — this shortcut's target no longer exists.
pin.gestureToast: Tip: single-click a shortcut to open it, double-click to run it.
pin.missing.message: Can't open {name} — the file no longer exists at {path}.
pin.relocateOutsideFolder: A project shortcut must stay inside its workspace folder. Add {name} as a global shortcut to point outside it.
group.added: Created group {name}. Drag shortcuts into it to organize them.
group.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder before adding a project group.
annotation.commentPrompt: Comment text to show in the shortcut list
annotation.separatorAdded: Added a separator to the shortcut list.
annotation.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder before adding a project comment or separator.
url.labelPlaceholder: e.g. Staging dashboard — leave empty to show the address
url.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder before adding a project website shortcut.
scanUrls.title: Add website shortcuts found in this project
scanUrls.placeholder: Pick the websites to add as project shortcuts
scanUrls.none: No project websites found. The git remote, package.json, and docs config declared no addresses to open.
scanUrls.allAdded: Every project website is already a shortcut.
scanUrls.added: Added {count} website shortcut(s) to Project Shortcuts.
group.deleteConfirm: Delete the group {name}? Its shortcuts move to the top level (they are not removed).
group.deleted: Deleted group {name}; moved {count} shortcut(s) to the top level.
configure.title: Configure Run: {name}
configure.hubPlaceholder: Pick a setting to edit, or Save to apply.
configure.autoUnsupported: Run parameters cannot be set on an auto-shortcut. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
configure.command.prompt: Command prefix placed before the file path (e.g. python, node). Tokens: $workspaceRoot, $dir, $file, $fileName, $fileNameWithoutExt. Ask at run time with ${prompt:Label} or ${pick:a,b,c}.
configure.command.placeholder: Leave empty to use the default for this file type
configure.args.prompt: Arguments appended after the file path. Wrap an argument with spaces in double quotes. Tokens: $workspaceRoot, $dir, $file, $fileName, $fileNameWithoutExt. Ask at run time with ${prompt:Label} or ${pick:a,b,c}.
configure.cwd.placeholder: Choose the working directory for the run
configure.cwd.customPrompt: Absolute path to the working directory.
configure.cwd.notFound: No directory exists at that path.
configure.env.placeholder: Add, edit, or remove an environment variable
configure.terminal.externalDetail: Open a separate OS terminal window outside VS Code.
configure.elevated.placeholder: Run the external window with administrator privileges?
configure.elevated.detail: Prompts for elevation (Windows UAC). Per-shortcut environment variables are not passed to an elevated window.
configure.extract.prompt: Regular expression matched against a background run's output when it finishes. The first capture group (or the whole match) is copied to your clipboard. Only applies to runs in the background output channel.
configure.extract.invalid: Enter a valid regular expression, or leave empty to clear.
configure.dependsOn.placeholder: Pick a shortcut that must succeed before this one runs, or clear it.
configure.sound.placeholder: Play audio cues for this shortcut?
configure.sound.on: Always (chime on every event for this shortcut)
configure.runOnSave.placeholder: Run this shortcut automatically when its file is saved?
configure.runOnSave.on: On (run when the file is saved)
configure.concurrency.placeholder: Allow this shortcut to run while one of its runs is already in progress?
configure.concurrency.blockChoice: Block - one run at a time (default)
configure.concurrency.blockDetail: A scheduled, chained, or saved run is skipped while a previous run is still in progress; a manual run asks first.
configure.concurrency.allowDetail: The shortcut may start again before a previous run finishes.
configure.lock.prompt: Lock name shared with other windows or scripts. While a live holder owns it, this shortcut will not start. Leave empty for none.
configure.fileArg.placeholder: Insert the shortcut file's path into the command?
configure.fileArg.off: No - run from args only (e.g. npm run build)
configureRun.title: Saropa Run: {name}
configureRun.subtitle: Set how {name} runs when you execute it. Every option is here; nothing is hidden behind a menu.
configureRun.label.fileArg: Pass the file path to the command
configureRun.label.fileArg.desc: On (default) runs the file: <command> <file> <args>. Off omits the file, for a run that names its work in args (npm run build, make test).
configureRun.elevated.desc: Prompts for elevation (Windows UAC; pkexec/sudo elsewhere). Per-shortcut environment variables are not passed to an elevated window.
configureRun.elevated.needsExternal: Only a new external window can run elevated. Set "Run in" to that to enable this.
configureRun.runOnSave.desc: Run this shortcut automatically every time its own file is saved.
configureRun.concurrency.desc: Off (default) is one run at a time: a scheduled, chained, or saved run is skipped while a previous run is still going. On lets runs overlap.
configureRun.cwd.placeholder: Leave empty to use the owning folder
configureRun.env.empty: No environment variables. They are merged over the inherited environment.
configureRun.footer.none: Opens the file (no run command for this file type).
simulate.title: Simulate Run: {name}
simulate.intro: Dry run — nothing was executed. This is what a real run would do.
simulate.notRunnable: {name} has no run command, so running it would open the file in the editor instead.
simulate.unknownNote: Left literal in the command (they may be intentional shell variables).
simulate.action.url: Opens this URL in your browser:
drop.notRunnable: {name} has no run command, so a dropped file has nothing to run. Set one with Configure Run.
depends.blocked: {name} is waiting on {dep}. Run {dep} successfully first.
diffRuns.needTwo: Run {name} in the background at least twice to compare its output.
diffRuns.title: {name}: previous vs latest run
share.copied: Copied a shareable link for {name} to the clipboard.
share.import.invalid: That Saropa import link is not valid.
share.import.noFolder: Open a workspace folder to import this shortcut as a project shortcut.
external.title: Add a file from anywhere as a shortcut
template.notFile: Only a file shortcut can be used as a template.
template.prompt: New name for the copy of {source} (any case style).
template.exists: A file named {name} already exists here.
duplicateArg.notFile: {name} is not a file shortcut, so it has no argument line to duplicate.
duplicateArg.title: Duplicate with Argument
duplicateArg.argsPrompt: Arguments the duplicate runs with. Wrap an argument with spaces in double quotes.
duplicateArg.namePrompt: Name for the duplicate. Defaults to the original name with the arguments added.
duplicateArg.failed: Could not duplicate {name} — it may have been removed.
fileOps.notFile: {name} is not a file shortcut, so there is no file to act on.
fileOps.newPrompt: Name for the new file, created next to this shortcut.
fileOps.exists: A file named {name} already exists here.
fileOps.created: Created and added {name} as a shortcut.
fileOps.copyNoName: Could not find a free name to duplicate {name}.
fileOps.renamePrompt: New file name. The shortcut follows the file to its new name.
fileOps.deleteDetail: The file is moved to your system trash, so it can be recovered.
fileOps.lockFailed: Could not change the read-only state of {name}: {error}
focus.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder before focusing on shortcut files.
focus.nothingToHide: Nothing to focus: no folder has shortcut files below its root to hide siblings of.
focus.entered: Focused the Explorer on your shortcut files ({count} entries hidden). Exit Focus to restore.
focus.exited: Restored the Explorer. Your previous file filters are back.
runTarget.title: Run targets in {name}
runTarget.placeholder: Pick how to run this shortcut, or press Escape to run the file directly.
runWith.notFile: Only a file shortcut runs through an interpreter.
runWith.title: Run {name} with
runWith.placeholder: Choose an interpreter — runtimes found on this machine are listed first
runWith.useDefault: Use the default for this file type
runWith.useDefault.detail: Clear the interpreter so the file-type default applies
runWith.runDirectly.detail: Let the file run itself via its shebang (macOS and Linux)
runWith.browse.detail: Pick an interpreter executable from disk
runWith.browse.title: Select an interpreter executable
configureRun.interp.defaultHint: Empty runs with {interpreter}, the default for this file type.
configureRun.interp.defaultHintNone: Empty opens the file — its type has no interpreter.
action.commandFailed: Couldn't run "{name}". The tool may not be installed or activated yet.
routine.notReady: [{name}] the routine engine is not ready yet; try again in a moment.
routine.empty: Routine "{name}" has no members to run.
routine.memberMissing: skipped: "{member}" — its recipe is not present in this folder.
routine.nestedSkipped: skipped: "{member}" — a routine cannot run another routine.
routine.interactiveSkipped: skipped: "{member}" — needs input, cannot run unattended.
routine.interactiveSkippedDetail: interactive; skipped in an unattended run
routine.new.needTwo: Select at least two shortcuts (Ctrl/Cmd-click) to compose into a routine.
routine.new.title: New routine from selection
routine.new.prompt: Name this routine ({count} members, run in sequence)
routine.new.nameEmpty: Enter a name for the routine.
routine.new.saved: Created routine "{name}" with {count} members. Double-click to run them in sequence.
routine.new.notSaved: Could not create the routine (no workspace folder for a project shortcut).
recipe.clickHint: Click for details; use the play button to run.
recipe.info.title: {name}
recipe.info.scheduled: Scheduled for {time} (disabled until promoted and enabled).
recipe.promoted: Promoted {name} to a shortcut you can edit.
recipe.scheduleEnabled: {name} enabled — its schedule is now on.
recommend.hint: New here? These are worth turning on.
recipe.config.none: No recognized config files to open.
recipe.version.none: No name and version found in the project manifest.
recipe.script.none: No package.json with scripts found near the active file.
monitor.panel.title: Saropa Dashboard
monitor.copied: Copied the toolchain process report to the clipboard.
monitor.kill.confirm: End {name} (PID {pid})? This terminates the process immediately.
monitor.kill.protected: {tool} processes are not killable from the monitor.
monitor.snapshot.noFolder: Open a folder to snapshot the toolchain.
monitor.snapshot.failed: Could not write the toolchain snapshot: {error}
monitor.heartbeat.ram: {tool} is using {rss} of RAM (over the {ceiling} ceiling).
hygiene.noFolder: Open a folder to scan for empty and oversized files.
hygiene.scanning: Scanning for empty and oversized files…
hygiene.clean: Hygiene scan: no issues across {files} files in {dirs} folders.
hygiene.failed: Could not write the hygiene report: {error}
hygiene.savedInvalid: This saved scan's configuration is invalid. Recreate it with New Hygiene Scan.
hygiene.new.title: New Hygiene Scan
hygiene.new.fileCeilingPrompt: Flag files larger than how many megabytes?
hygiene.new.folderCeilingPrompt: Flag folders whose total exceeds how many megabytes?
hygiene.new.numberInvalid: Enter a number of megabytes greater than zero.
hygiene.new.name: Hygiene: {scope} ({detail})
hygiene.new.saved: Saved scan shortcut {name}. Double-click it to run, or schedule it.
hygiene.new.notSaved: Could not save the scan shortcut (no workspace folder for a project shortcut).
bloat.noFolder: Open a folder to scan for workspace bloat.
bloat.scanning: Scanning for oversized directories VS Code crawls on open…
bloat.clean: Workspace bloat scan: {count} project(s) clean — nothing oversized or unguarded.
bloat.found: Workspace bloat scan found {count} finding(s) VS Code crawls on open.
bloat.failed: Could not write the workspace bloat report: {error}
bloat.guardForeign: {project} is not the open workspace. Its remediation is in the report; apply it in that project yourself.
bloat.guardUnparseable: Could not safely update {path} (it has comments or syntax this action can't round-trip). Add the files.watcherExclude entry by hand.
bloat.guarded: Added {count} files.watcherExclude entr(ies) to {project}. Reload the window to stop crawling them.
bloat.guardedNoop: {project} already excludes those directories from the watcher.
bloat.pruneAbsent: No .vscode-test cache to prune in this project.
bloat.pruneMessage: Delete {project}'s .vscode-test cache and reclaim {size}? The test runner re-downloads what it needs next run.
runAny.placeholder: Run a shortcut by name (recently run first).
runAny.empty: No shortcuts to run yet. Add a file as a shortcut first.
runTop.noPin: No shortcut in position {slot}. Add or reorder shortcuts to fill it.
override.pickPlaceholder: Pick a shortcut to run with one-off overrides.
override.title: Run with overrides: {name}
override.argsPrompt: Arguments for this run only (the stored shortcut is unchanged).
override.cwdPrompt: Working directory for this run only. Leave empty for the default.
override.envPrompt: Environment for this run only, as KEY=value pairs separated by spaces.
suggest.prompt: You have opened {name} {count} times. Add it as a shortcut for quick access?
suggest.ignored: No longer suggesting {ext} files. Change this under saropaWorkspace.suggestions.ignoreExtensions.
suggest.ignoreFailed: Could not save the setting to ignore {ext} files.
tabSuggest.prompt: You've kept {name} pinned as a tab for over {hours}h. Add it to your Saropa shortcuts?
tabSuggest.restored: Restored {count} dismissed pinned-tab suggestion(s). Tabs kept pinned past the threshold can be offered again.
appearance.title: Icon & Color: {name}
appearance.autoUnsupported: An icon cannot be set on an auto-shortcut. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
appearance.group.title: Group Icon & Color: {name}
appearance.group.saved: Updated the icon for the {name} group.
appearance.icon.placeholder: Pick an icon by category, or type to search.

appearance.iconKeyword.code: source script braces brackets programming develop
appearance.iconKeyword.rocket: launch deploy ship start boost release

customize.title: Saropa Customize: {name}
customize.subtitle: Set the name, icon, color, and tags for {name} — all on one screen.
customize.section.name: Name
customize.name.desc: The label shown in the tree. Leave empty to use the file name.
customize.icon.search: Search icons by name or keyword
customize.color.desc: Tints the chosen icon. Each color is tuned for light, dark, and high-contrast themes.
customize.tags.desc: Tags drive the mode filter in the Shortcuts view. Add one and press Enter.
customize.tags.placeholder: Add a tag and press Enter
metric.title: Live Metric: {name}
metric.autoUnsupported: A live metric cannot be set on an auto-shortcut. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
metric.fileOnly: A live metric can only be set on a file shortcut.
metric.kindPlaceholder: Pick what to show on this shortcut, or turn it off.
metric.kind.sizeDetail: Show the file's size, and optionally warn when it grows past a limit.
metric.kind.linesDetail: Show the number of lines (large files fall back to size).
metric.kind.modifiedDetail: Show how long ago the file last changed.
metric.kind.offDetail: Remove the live metric from this shortcut.
metric.thresholdPrompt: Warn when the file grows past this size (leave blank for no limit).
metric.thresholdInvalid: Enter a size like 250kb, 5mb, or 1gb (or leave blank).
metric.overTooltip: Live metric: {value} — over its size limit.
schedule.title: Configure Schedule: {name}
schedule.hubPlaceholder: Pick a setting to edit, or Save to apply.
scheduleEditor.title: Saropa Workspace Scheduler: {name}
scheduleEditor.subtitle: Set when {name} runs on its own.
scheduleEditor.gone: That shortcut no longer exists, so its schedule was not saved.
scheduleEditor.daily.desc: Run at a set time each day. Leave empty for no daily run.
scheduleEditor.days.needsTime: Set a daily time first to choose days.
scheduleEditor.repeat.desc: Run on a fixed interval, in addition to any daily time.
scheduleEditor.cron.desc: For schedules a time and interval can't express. Uses a 5-field cron expression.
scheduleEditor.cron.invalid: Not a valid cron expression — this won't run until it's fixed.
scheduleEditor.runOnStartup.desc: Also run shortly after you open this folder.
scheduleEditor.catchUp.desc: If a run was due while VS Code was closed, run it once when you next open this folder.
scheduleEditor.enabled.desc: Turn the whole schedule on or off without losing it.
scheduleEditor.around.desc: How this shortcut's daily time sits with your other scheduled shortcuts.
scheduleEditor.insight.conflict: Also runs at {time}: {names}. They'll start together.
scheduleEditor.insight.alone: No other shortcuts run at a set daily time.
scheduleEditor.insight.empty: Set a daily time to see how it fits your day.
scheduleEditor.nextRun.disabled: Disabled — turn Enabled on to run it
scheduleEditor.savedDisabled: Saved schedule for {name} (disabled — turn Enabled on to run it).
schedule.autoUnsupported: A schedule cannot be set on an auto-shortcut. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
expiry.autoUnsupported: An auto-shortcut cannot be time-bombed. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
expiry.pick.title: Shortcut Until: {name}
expiry.pick.placeholder: When should this shortcut remove itself?
expiry.custom.title: Shortcut Until a Custom Date
expiry.custom.prompt: Enter a date (and optional time). The shortcut removes itself after it.
expiry.custom.invalid: Enter a valid date as YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm.
expiry.noRepo: {name}: no workspace folder to read a git branch from.
expiry.noBranch: {name}: could not read the current git branch (no repo, or a detached / unreadable HEAD).
expiry.branchSet: {name} will remove itself when you leave branch {branch}.
expiry.tooltip.branch: Removes itself when you leave branch {branch}
expiry.restored: Restored {count} shortcut(s); the expiry was cleared.
schedule.atTime.prompt: Time of day to run, 24-hour HH:mm. Leave empty for no daily run.
schedule.days.placeholder: Pick the days the daily time runs on (none = every day)
schedule.interval.placeholder: How often should this shortcut repeat?
schedule.interval.customPrompt: Repeat every N of the chosen unit.
schedule.interval.invalid: Enter a whole number greater than zero.
schedule.unit.placeholder: Choose a unit for the custom interval
schedule.cron.placeholder: Build a cron schedule, or type one directly
schedule.cron.preset.weeklyAt: $(calendar) On a weekday each week at a time...
schedule.cron.preset.monthlyAt: $(calendar) On the 1st of each month at a time...
schedule.cron.preset.workHours: $(watch) Every few minutes during work hours (Mon-Fri 9-5)...
schedule.cron.timePrompt: Time of day to run, 24-hour HH:mm.
schedule.cron.advancedPrompt: 5-field cron: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. Leave empty to clear.
schedule.cron.invalid: Enter a valid 5-field cron expression (e.g. 0 9 * * 1-5), or leave empty to clear.
statusBar.tooltip: Next scheduled run: {name} at {time}. Click for its report, schedule, and hide options.
statusBar.name: Next scheduled run
statusBar.actions.title: {name} runs next at {time}
statusBar.actions.placeholder: Choose an action for this scheduled run
statusBar.actions.openScheduleDetail: Every scheduled item, its last outcome, and a link to its latest report
statusBar.actions.disableDetail: Stops the automatic run. The shortcut stays, and you can still run it manually.
statusBar.actions.disabled: Schedule turned off for {name}. Run it manually from the Shortcuts view.
statusBar.actions.hideDetail: Hides the status-bar item only. Scheduled runs continue.
statusBar.actions.hidden: Next-run indicator hidden. Turn it back on in Settings: Saropa Workspace > Show Schedule Status Bar.
pause.tooltip: Paused: automatic runs (schedule, triggers, on save) are suspended. A manual run still works.
pause.paused: Paused {name}. It will not run automatically until you unpause it.
schedule.missing: [{time}] Scheduled run skipped: {name} - file not found.
schedule.interactiveSkipped: [{time}] Scheduled run skipped: {name} needs interactive input (${prompt} / ${pick}).
schedule.missed.offer: {count} scheduled runs were missed while VS Code was closed.
schedule.missed.offerOne: {name} was due while VS Code was closed.
schedulePanel.title: Saropa Scheduled Runs
schedulePanel.heading: Scheduled shortcuts
schedulePanel.empty: No shortcut has an enabled schedule yet. Set one from a shortcut's Configure Schedule menu.
schedulePanel.reportGone: That report is no longer available.
concurrency.reasonRunning: a previous run is still in progress
concurrency.reasonLocked: its lock is held by another process
lints.notInstalled: Saropa Lints is not installed, so there is no Code Health score to read. Install the saropa.saropa-lints extension to use it.
lints.noData: Saropa Lints has no analysis data yet. Run an analysis to compute the Code Health score?
lints.analysisFailed: Saropa Lints analysis did not complete. Open the Saropa Lints output for details.
lints.stillNoData: Analysis finished but no violations data was produced.
lints.scoreUnavailable: Saropa Lints has data, but not a full enough sweep to score yet (a partial/incremental analysis). Run a full analysis to get the score.
lints.score: Code Health {score}/100 - {band}. {errors} error(s), {warnings} warning(s), {infos} info across {files} file(s).
stats.noFolder: Open a workspace folder to collect project stats.
stats.failed: Could not write the project stats report: {error}
stats.done: Project stats: {languages} language(s), {files} file(s), {lines} line(s). Report opened.
deps.noFolder: Open a workspace folder to check dependency freshness.
deps.done: Pubspec freshness: {count} package(s) behind latest. Report opened.
planner.title: Saropa Schedule & Workflow Planner
planner.subtitle: Day and week timelines, plus a graph of chained and event-triggered shortcuts. Drag to retime; link shortcuts to chain them.
planner.linked: Linked {name}. It will auto-run when the source fires.
planner.detail.label: Selected item details
planner.detail.resize: Drag to resize the details panel
planner.scheduleResumed: Resumed {name}. Its schedule will fire again.
planner.schedulePaused: Paused {name}. It will not run on its own until resumed.
chain.cooldown: [Chain] Skipped {name} ({cause}) - cooling down to avoid a trigger loop.
watch.cooldown: [Watch] Skipped {name} ({file} changed) - cooling down to avoid a run storm.

watch.addFileDetail: Pick a file; saving it runs this shortcut.
watch.globPrompt: Glob pattern (workspace-relative). Saving a matching file runs {name} in the background.
watch.saved: {name} runs when a watched path changes: {globs}.
folderWatch.newFilesStartup: {count} new in {label} while you were away: {files}
folderWatch.changedFilesStartup: {count} changed in {label} while you were away: {files}
folderWatch.mixedFilesStartup: {added} new, {changed} changed in {label} while you were away: {files}
folderWatch.tooManyFiles: [Watch] Stopped scanning {dir} at {max} files; narrow the watch with a glob.
folderWatch.pickFolderTitle: Pick a folder to watch for new files
folderWatch.pickFileTitle: Pick a file to watch for changes
folderWatch.modeTitle: What should this folder watch report?
folderWatch.modePlaceholder: Pick what to be told about.
folderWatch.modeNewDetail: Get a toast when a file appears in this folder.
folderWatch.modeChangedDetail: Get a toast when a file appears or is edited.
folderWatch.globPrompt: Glob to match files within the folder. Leave empty to watch every file.

folderWatch.addedChanged: Watching {name} for new and changed files, including changes made while this window was closed.
folderWatch.none: No folder or file watches yet. Add one from a folder's right-click menu or the command palette.
folderWatch.managePlaceholder: Pick a watch to enable, disable, or remove.

folderWatch.actionPlaceholder: Pick an action for this watch.
folderWatch.suggestBugs: {folder} has a bugs folder. Watch it for new files? You will be told when a new report lands, even one written while this window is closed.
folderWatch.alertHereOn: Now alerting in this project for {name}.
folderWatch.alertHereOff: Stopped alerting in this project for {name}.
folderWatch.noProjectOpen: Open a project folder first - a watch alerts per project.
watchesView.rowTooltip:
{target}
Watching this {kind} for {mode}. {scope} Click to open what changed and clear the count.
watchesView.openTitle: Open watch and clear its count
watchesView.badgeTooltip: {count} new or changed files across your watches
chain.idleInteractiveSkipped: [Chain] Skipped {name} on idle - it needs interactive input (${prompt} / ${pick}) and cannot run unattended.
triggers.title: Triggers - {name}
triggers.autoUnsupported: Auto-shortcuts cannot carry triggers. Promote it to a shortcut first.
triggers.emits.placeholder: Mark the events this shortcut's completion should fire
triggers.empty: No triggers yet - this shortcut runs only manually or on its schedule.
triggers.successOnly.off: $(circle-large-outline) Runs on any completion (select to require success)
triggers.addPin.none: No other shortcuts to chain from.
triggers.addPin.placeholder: Run this shortcut after which shortcut completes?
triggers.addEvent.placeholder: Run this shortcut after which event?
triggers.idle.prompt: Run this shortcut after how many minutes of no VS Code interaction? It runs once in the background each time you step away.
triggers.idle.invalid: Enter a whole number of minutes greater than 0.
triggers.eventDetail.build: Runs when a shortcut marked as a build step finishes.
triggers.eventDetail.publish: Runs when a shortcut marked as a publish step finishes.
triggers.eventDetail.gitCommit: Runs when a commit is recorded in this repo.
triggers.eventDetail.gitPush: Runs when a push updates a remote-tracking branch.
triggers.emitDetail.build: Other shortcuts can run after a build by triggering on it.
triggers.emitDetail.publish: Other shortcuts can run after a publish by triggering on it.
run.runningTooltip: Running in the background. Use Stop to terminate.
run.stoppingTooltip: Stopping... it will be force-killed if it does not exit. Use Force Kill to terminate now.
run.notRunning: {name} is not running in the background.
run.alreadyRunning: {name} is already running. Stop it first, run another anyway, or view its output.
run.alreadyLocked: {name} is locked by another process (lock "{lock}", pid {pid}). Run anyway, or view output.
run.openedNotRunnable: {name} has no run command, so it was opened. Use Configure Run to make it runnable.
run.externalStarted: Launched {name} in a new external window.
run.externalElevatedStarted: Launched {name} in a new administrator window (approve the elevation prompt).
run.externalFailed: Couldn't open an external window for {name}: {error}
run.elevatedEnvDropped: {name} ran elevated, so its custom environment variables were not applied.
portUnwedge.blocked: Port {port} is held by {process} (PID {pid}), so {name} couldn't start.
portUnwedge.blockedUnknown: Port {port} is in use, so {name} couldn't start, but the process holding it couldn't be identified.
portUnwedge.confirmBody: Kill {process} (PID {pid}) holding port {port}? This stops that process immediately.
portUnwedge.killFailed: Couldn't kill {process} (PID {pid}) — it may need elevated permissions. Port {port} is still in use.
import.none: No favorites from other extensions were found.
import.doneWithSkips: Imported {count} shortcut(s) from {file}. Skipped {skipped} entry(ies) — open the output channel for details.
import.nothingNew: No new shortcuts to import from {file}.
import.log.skipFolder: [Import] {file}: skipped folder favorite "{name}" — a folder has no shortcut equivalent (its grouped files were imported).
import.log.skipNoPath: [Import] {file}: skipped an entry with no file path.
import.log.skipBlankPath: [Import] {file}: skipped a line with no path.
import.log.skipUnresolved: [Import] {key}: skipped {path} — no workspace folder to resolve a relative path against.
import.log.skipOutsideFolder: [Import] {file}: skipped {path} — it is outside the workspace folder.
import.log.skipUnsupported: [Import] {file}: skipped "{name}" — this item type has no shortcut equivalent.
import.prompt: Found {file} from another favorites extension. Import its {count} file(s) as project shortcuts?
import.sibling.none: No favorites files found in sibling projects.
import.sibling.placeholder: Select sibling projects to import favorites from (added as global shortcuts).
import.sibling.done: Imported {count} shortcut(s) from sibling projects as global shortcuts.
import.sibling.nothingNew: No new shortcuts to import from the selected sibling projects.
pinSet.statusBarTooltip: Active shortcut set: {name} ({count} set(s)). Click to switch or manage sets.
pinSet.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder to use shortcut sets.
pinSet.switch.placeholder: Switch to a shortcut set, or manage sets.
pinSet.nameEmpty: Enter a name for the shortcut set.
pinSet.nameExists: A shortcut set named {name} already exists.
pinSet.created: Switched to new shortcut set {name}. Its project shortcuts start empty; global shortcuts stay shared across all sets.
pinSet.duplicate.prompt: Name for the copy of shortcut set {name}
pinSet.duplicated: Duplicated shortcut set {source} to {name} and switched to it.
pinSet.delete.confirm: Delete shortcut set {name}? Its project shortcuts are removed. Global shortcuts are unaffected.
pinSet.delete.lastOne: {name} is the only shortcut set; create another before deleting it.
export.empty: There are no shortcuts to export in the chosen scope.
import.set.invalid: That file is not a Saropa shortcut set, or its version is unsupported.
import.set.nothing: The shortcut set was empty for the chosen scope.
pinsConfig.title: Edit Shortcuts Config
pinsConfig.noFolder: Open a workspace folder to edit its shortcuts config.
pinsConfig.pickFolder: Pick the workspace folder whose shortcuts config to edit.
projectFiles.tooltipPinned: Added as a shortcut to this project. Use the shortcut button to remove it.
untapped.rowTooltip: You haven't opened or run this shortcut yet. Open or run it to clear the dot.
telemetry.resetConfirm: Clear the local run history? This removes the Recent list and run counts kept on this machine. It cannot be undone.
analytics.title: Run Analytics
analytics.intro: Local only — built from run history kept on this machine, never transmitted.
analytics.disabled: Run history collection is off. Turn on saropaWorkspace.telemetry.enabled to collect analytics.
analytics.empty: No runs recorded yet. Run a shortcut to start collecting local analytics.
analytics.sessionNote: Background-run outcomes from the current session (cleared on reload).
dailyReport.title: Saropa Suite Daily Report — {date}
dailyReport.intro: Local only — Workspace run history plus daily summaries from the installed Saropa Suite extensions, all read on this machine and never transmitted.
dailyReport.troubleEmpty: No failures or high-impact signals today.
dailyReport.workspaceDisabled: Run history collection is off. Turn on saropaWorkspace.telemetry.enabled to include Workspace activity in the report.
dailyReport.noSiblings: No other Saropa Suite extensions reported data. Install Saropa Log Capture, Saropa Lints, or Saropa Drift Advisor to see their daily summaries here.
dailyReport.noFolder: Open a folder to write the Saropa Suite daily report — the report file lives under the workspace's reports directory.
dailyReport.versionMismatch: {tool} is installed but reports its data in a newer format than this version of Saropa Workspace understands — update Saropa Workspace to include its section.
dailyReport.writeFailed: The Saropa Suite daily report could not be written: {error}
routine.note.missing: Shortcut not found — it was removed or renamed since this routine was configured. Edit the routine to re-link or remove this member.
routine.note.failedExit: exit code {code} — see this member's section below or the output channel for details.
routine.summary.readFailed: This member's report could not be read — open the link above.
routine.summary.noReports: The routine ran; no member produced a report to show.
dashboard.sampling: Sampling (≈1s for a live CPU delta)…
dashboard.processEmpty: No detected toolchain processes are running.
trends.noCpu: No heartbeat samples yet. Enable the toolchain heartbeat to chart CPU over time.
trends.noDebt: No tech-debt reports yet. Run the Tech-debt harvest ritual to chart markers over time.
trends.noReports: No scheduled reports yet. Promote and run a scheduled recipe to populate this list.
boot.configure.title: Workspace Boot Sequence
boot.configure.placeholder: Add or arrange the shortcuts that run when this workspace opens.
boot.add.placeholder: Pick shortcuts to add (they run in the order shown).
boot.add.none: All your shortcuts are already in the boot sequence.
boot.saved: Saved the workspace boot sequence ({count} step(s)).
boot.prompt: Run this workspace's boot sequence? {count} step(s) will open files and run scripts.
boot.run.empty: The workspace boot sequence is empty. Add shortcuts with Configure Workspace Boot Sequence.
boot.run.missing: [Boot] Step {index}: shortcut no longer exists; skipped.
boot.run.stopped: [Boot] Stopped after a failed step (stop-on-error is on).
boot.run.done: Boot sequence finished: ran {ran} of {total} step(s).
filter.message: Filter: {summary} — {hidden} hidden. Clear it from the view toolbar.
filter.input.title: Filter Shortcuts
filter.input.prompt: Type to filter by name, path, or command. Use the buttons to limit to Scripts, Files, or Failed runs.
filter.button.scriptsOn: Scripts only is on — click to clear
filter.button.filesOn: Files only is on — click to clear
filter.button.failedOn: Failed runs only is on — click to clear
tag.title: Tags: {name}
tag.placeholder: Pick tags for this shortcut, or add a new one.
tag.newPrompt: New tag(s), separated by spaces or commas (the leading # is optional).
tag.autoUnsupported: Tags can't be set on an auto or recipe shortcut. Add the file as a shortcut explicitly first.
branch.unsupported: {name} is an auto/recipe shortcut and cannot be linked to a branch.
branch.noRepo: Cannot link {name}: no git repository is open.
branch.noBranch: Cannot read a git branch for {name}; it stays on all branches.
branchSet.switched: Switched to shortcut set {set} for branch {branch}.
branchSet.switchedAndRan: Switched to shortcut set {set} for branch {branch}; running {name}.
branchSet.pinMissing: [Branch sets] On branch {branch}: activated set {set}, but its on-switch shortcut no longer exists; skipped.
branchSet.noBranch: No git branch to link: open a repository on a branch (a detached or unreadable HEAD has no branch to bind).
branchSet.link.setPlaceholder: Pick the shortcut set to activate on branch {branch}.
branchSet.link.pinPlaceholder: Optionally run a shortcut when switching to {set}, or pick None.
branchSet.link.noPin: $(circle-slash) No shortcut — just switch the set
branchSet.linked: Branch {branch} now activates shortcut set {set} on checkout.
branchSet.linkedDisabled: Linked branch {branch} to shortcut set {set}. Turn on branch-aware switching to apply it.
branchSet.unlink.none: Branch {branch} is not linked to a shortcut set.
mode.title: Filter Shortcuts by Tag
mode.placeholder: Pick a tag to show only its shortcuts, or show all.
mode.noTags: No tags yet. Right-click a shortcut and choose Tag Shortcut first.
mode.set: Showing only {tag} shortcuts. Clear the tag filter to show all.
mode.cleared: Cleared the tag filter. Showing all shortcuts.
scripts.run.missingEntry: Script {name} not found — its entry file is missing from the extension.
scripts.run.noWorkspace: Open a workspace folder to run {name} — the script needs a project folder to act on.
scripts.run.notFound: Script not found in the library.
scripts.run.missingRequirement: {name} needs {list} on PATH — install it and try again.
scripts.organizeOutput.label: Organize output folder
scripts.organizeOutput.description: Sort a folder's loose files into dated YYYY.MM/YYYY.MM.DD subfolders and remove the empty folders left behind. Asks for the folder to organize (e.g. logs or reports) and refuses a project/repository root; files still being written, hidden files, and already-sorted files are left alone.
scripts.deviceConnect.label: Connect a device for debugging
scripts.deviceConnect.description: Connect a physical Android device to Flutter for on-device debugging over Wi-Fi or USB, mirror the screen with scrcpy, and report battery, charging, and connectivity health. Runs an interactive menu in the terminal from the open project folder. Needs the rich, plyer, and zeroconf Python packages; the script asks before installing any that are missing.
